response:
THE LAW OF MOORE, FORMULATED BY GORDON ECKERT MOORE IN 1965, PREDICTS THAT THE NUMBER OF TRANSISTORS ON A MICROPROCESSOR WILL DOUBLE ABOUT EVERY TWO YEARS. THIS TRENDS HAS DRIVEN THE RAPID ADVANCEMENT OF COMPUTING TECHNOLOGY FOR DECADES. HOWEVER, AS WE APPROACH THE PHYSICAL LIMITS OF SILICON BASED TECHNOLOGY, IT IS BECOMING INCREASINGLY DIFFICULT TO CONTINUE THIS EXPONENTIAL GROWTH. SCIENTISTS AND ENGINEERS ARE NOW EXPLORING ALTERNATIVE MATERIALS AND NEW ARCHITECTURES TO SUSTAIN THE INNOVATION THAT THE LAW OF MOORE HAS ENABLED.

***
WHILE IT IS DIFFICULT TO PREDICT THE EXACT POINT AT WHICH THE LAW OF MOORE WILL CEASE TO BE VALID, MANY EXPERTS BELIEVE THAT WE MAY HAVE REACHED A TIPPING POINT IN THE NEXT DECADE. THE INCREASING COST AND COMPLEXITY OF MANUFACTURING SMALLER AND SMALLER TRANSISTORS ARE MAKING IT LESS FEASIBLE TO FOLLOW THE LAW'S PREDICTION. INSTEAD OF A CONTINUOUS EXPONENTIAL GROWTH, THE FUTURE MAY SEE A SHIFT TOWARD MORE EFFICIENT AND SPECIALIZED COMPUTING SOLUTIONS. DO
